page: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-2 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled, providing 2 threads per core. The system is equipped with 2.0 GB of RAM, yielding a resource density of 1.0 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include bundled local storage or GPUs, and it offers zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations indicate that the server delivers average single-core performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1399.29 and a bzip3 single-threaded decompression rate of 8.87 GB/sec. However, multi-core and memory-intensive operations show limitations. The stress-ng multi-core score is 1841.88, and the Geekbench multi-core score is 1311.0. Memory bandwidth is measured at 13.72 GB/sec for read-only large blocks and 11.78 GB/sec for write large blocks. Redis SET operations achieve 1.75 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput reaches 2.06 GB/sec.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation and low memory-to-vCPU ratio, this instance represents a compute-focused entry point within the Intel Xeon-based N2 family. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that require dedicated CPU cycles but have minimal memory footprints. Due to the 2.0 GB RAM constraint and lower multi-core benchmark results, it is best suited for lightweight single-threaded tasks, basic utility scripting, or small-scale background workers rather than memory-heavy databases or complex multi-threaded applications.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-2 is an x86_64 compute-focused server featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs from 1 physical Intel Xeon core running at 2.8 GHz, paired with 2.0 GB of RAM. It does not include local storage or GPUs. Performance benchmarks show average single-core capabilities, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1399.29 and a bzip3 single-threaded decompression rate of 8.87 GB/sec. However, multi-core and memory-bound metrics are limited, as evidenced by a Geekbench multi-core score of 1311.0 and a large-block memory read bandwidth of 13.72 GB/sec. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for dedicated CPU workloads with low memory requirements. It is best suited for lightweight single-threaded tasks, basic utility scripts, and small background workers.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-2 is an x86_64 server with 2 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 2.0 GB of RAM. It is designed for lightweight, compute-focused workloads requiring dedicated CPU allocation.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-2 is an x86_64 server featuring 2 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 2.0 GB of RAM for compute-focused workloads.
tagline: A dedicated dual-vCPU compute instance with minimal memory footprint, optimized for lightweight single-threaded processing and utility workloads.
bullet_points: Features 2 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.8 GHz
Equipped with 2.0 GB of RAM, providing a 1:1 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Delivers average single-core performance for basic processing tasks
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for lightweight single-threaded tasks and background workers
